title: DAVID — Elizabeth I Tudor English Pronunciation Proof (Tilbury 1588)
concept: 480p marquee proof — DAVID speaks attested Tudor English with code-burned labels, IPA, sources card

config.voice_suffix: mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only

sources.citation: Elizabeth I: Collected Works (University of Chicago Press) — Tilbury speech, 9 August 1588

corpus.attested_text: I know I have the body of a weak and feeble woman; but I have the heart and stomach of a king, and of a king of England too.
corpus.attested_text_spoken: I know I have the body of a weak and feeble woman, but I have the heart and stomach of a king, and of a king of England too.
corpus.translation: I know I have the body of a weak and feeble woman; but I have the heart and stomach of a king, and of a king of England too.
corpus.pronunciation_model: reconstructed Early Modern English (Tudor court register, c. 1588)
corpus.line_ipa: [aɪ nəʊ aɪ hæv ðə ˈbɒdi əv ə wiːk ənd ˈfiːbl ˈwʊmən, bət aɪ hæv ðə hɑːt ənd ˈstʌmək əv ə kɪŋ, ənd əv ə kɪŋ əv ˈɪŋɡlənd tuː]
corpus.text_source: Elizabeth I, Tilbury address, 9 August 1588; Camden / Collected Works

The Archive host speaks slowly in reconstructed Tudor English (Early Modern pronunciation, c. 1588), lip-synced, delivering Elizabeth I's attested Tilbury line: "I know I have the body of a weak and feeble woman, but I have the heart and stomach of a king, and of a king of England too." Speech MUST be period Tudor English not modern commentary. Medium shot at worktable, warm brass lamp 3200K, documentary gravitas. mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, synthetic host only.
